title: East-West All-Star Game vs. MLB All-Star Game Attendance, 1933-1948
source: Larry Lester, Black Baseball's National Showcase: The East-West All-Star Game, 1933-1962 (expanded ed., 2020). Baseball Almanac. SABR Retrosheet.
methodology: East-West figures from Lester primary text as canonical source. MLB figures from Baseball Almanac and individual game Wikipedia articles, cross-referenced against SABR Retrosheet. Where the spec documents SABR cross-reference verification, those figures are used. East-West figures marked 'estimated' reflect round-number contemporary newspaper estimates rather than official gate counts. Some years had two East-West games; the Comiskey Park game is used as the canonical figure per Lester convention. 1945 MLB All-Star Game was canceled due to wartime travel restrictions.

confidence_vocabulary.Documented: Figure verified from Lester primary text and SABR Retrosheet cross-reference
confidence_vocabulary.Verified: Figure confirmed from at least two independent sources
confidence_vocabulary.Estimated: Contemporary newspaper estimate, typically a round number

notes.two_game_years_ew: In 1939, 1942, 1946, 1947, and 1948 two East-West games were played. The primary Comiskey Park game is used as the canonical attendance figure. Second-game attendance is noted separately.
notes.1944_mlb_discrepancy: The spec's SABR cross-reference states 29,598. Wikipedia and Baseball Almanac state 29,589. Both figures are noted; the SABR cross-reference figure is used as primary.
notes.1946_mlb_discrepancy: The spec's SABR cross-reference states 34,908. Wikipedia states 34,906. Both figures are noted; the SABR cross-reference figure is used as primary.
notes.1941_ew_discrepancy: Lester's primary text reports 50,256. Wikipedia states 50,246. The Lester figure is canonical.
notes.1942_ew_discrepancy: SABR cross-reference confirms 48,400. Wikipedia states 45,179 for the Comiskey game. The SABR/Lester figure is canonical.
notes.1946_ew_discrepancy: SABR cross-reference confirms 45,747. Wikipedia states 45,474 for the Comiskey game. The SABR/Lester figure is canonical.

data.mlb_note: First MLB All-Star Game. Jul 6, 1933. Tribune contemporary estimate was 49,000; official gate count is 47,595.

data.ew_note: Aug 26, 1934. Round number suggests newspaper estimate.
data.mlb_note: Jul 10, 1934. Wikipedia list page shows 48,368; individual game page shows 48,363.

data.ew_note: Aug 11, 1935. Round number suggests newspaper estimate.
Aug 23, 1936. First year East-West outdrew MLB ASG.
Aug 8, 1937. Round number suggests newspaper estimate.
Aug 21, 1938. Round number suggests newspaper estimate.
Aug 6, 1939. Second game held Aug 27 at Yankee Stadium (~20,000). Round number suggests newspaper estimate.
Aug 18, 1940. Round number suggests newspaper estimate.
Jul 27, 1941. Second-highest East-West attendance on record. Lester: 50,256. Wikipedia: 50,246.
Aug 16, 1942. Spec-verified SABR cross-reference: 48,400. Wikipedia lists 45,179 for Comiskey game. Second game held Aug 18 at Municipal Stadium, Cleveland (10,791).
Aug 1, 1943. Record East-West attendance. Gap of 19,785 over MLB ASG.

data.mlb_note: MLB All-Star Game not held in 1945 due to World War II wartime travel restrictions.

data.ew_note: Aug 18, 1946. SABR cross-reference: 45,747. Wikipedia: 45,474 for Comiskey game. Second game held Aug 15 at Griffith Stadium, Washington D.C. (16,268).
Jul 27, 1947. Second game held Jul 29 at Polo Grounds, New York (38,402).
Aug 22, 1948. Second game held Aug 24 at Yankee Stadium, New York (17,928).
